creator: freebsd@hjkos.com
text:
Hi, I come here from TrueNAS's bugtracker: https://jira.ixsystems.com/browse/NAS-113168

After updating FreeNAS 11.3 (FreeBSD 11.x?) to TrueNAS 12.0 (FreeBSD 12.2-RELEASE-p10) i've begun to have issues with my network adapter. I've been using a D-Link DUB-1312 which is a USB3 Ethernet adapter based on ASIX AX88179 chipset.

One issue is that my jails that are using VNET can't access most of the internet - most HTTPS requests hang after connecting, some HTTP requests hang when downloading data, jails themselves are accessible from LAN however. Jails that aren't under VNET work fine.

Another issue is that sometimes network adapter would sometimes spontaneously die, requiring a full reboot to recover, at least TrueNAS's command line says "Restarting interfaces: failed" or something when I try to recover it.

I'm using this USB3 interface because on this setup it's either USB or onboard realtek ethernet adapter which had massive stability issues on FreeNAS 11. I can't use a PCIe NIC since the only slot on this MiniITX mobo is occupied by SATA card.
